seo.title: OpenAI Assistants API Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for OpenAI Assistants API agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using OpenAI Assistants API face heightened compliance risks due to the high-stakes nature of hiring decisions and strict regulations like EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act. Built-in tools like code interpreter and file search can inadvertently introduce bias or discriminate against protected characteristics, leading to lawsuits and fines up to $1,500 per violation. Vex provides essential guardrails by verifying Assistant outputs against compliance schemas on run completion.

complianceRequirements.description: AI hiring tools must not discriminate based on race, gender, age, or other protected classes and require documentation of fair decision-making processes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex schemas validate candidate rankings and screening outputs to ensure no protected characteristics influence scores, flagging biased reasoning chains.

complianceRequirements.description: Employers must notify candidates when AI is used in hiring and conduct annual bias audits on screening algorithms.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs verification traces for audit compliance and checks Assistant responses for required disclosure language in candidate communications.

complianceRequirements.description: High-risk AI systems in employment must undergo risk assessments, ensure transparency, and prevent discriminatory outcomes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces transparency schemas requiring explanations of AI decisions and blocks outputs violating non-discrimination rules.

complianceRequirements.description: AI tools must accommodate disabilities and avoid disparate impact on protected groups in resume screening.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies that screening criteria do not penalize disability-related terms or proxies for protected characteristics.

industryRisks.name: Biased Resume Ranking
industryRisks.description: Code interpreter analyzes resumes and assigns scores correlated with gender indicators like 'PTA president'.
industryRisks.example: Assistant ranks male candidates higher due to hallucinated 'leadership' patterns from training data.

industryRisks.name: Irrelevant File Search
industryRisks.description: File search retrieves outdated diversity policies, causing contradictory screening advice.
industryRisks.example: Assistant cites 2015 policy excluding certain nationalities, violating current EEOC rules.

industryRisks.name: Context Overflow Bias
industryRisks.description: Thread grows beyond limits, truncating recent unbiased instructions and reverting to biased defaults.
industryRisks.example: Early thread instructions to 'prefer cultural fit' override later non-discrimination mandates.

industryRisks.name: Function Call Hallucination
industryRisks.description: Assistant fabricates arguments outside schema, like inventing protected class filters.

implementation.title: Webhook Setup for Run Completion
implementation.description: Configure OpenAI Assistants to call Vex webhook on 'run.completed' events, verifying screening outputs against HR compliance schema.
implementation.code:
import openai
from vex_sdk import VexClient

client = openai.OpenAI()
vex = VexClient(api_key='vex-key')

assistant = client.beta.assistants.create(
    name="HR Screener",
    instructions="Screen resumes per EEOC guidelines",
    tools=[...],
    tool_resources={"code_interpreter": {}, "file_search": {...}}
)

# Webhook configuration
webhook_url = vex.create_webhook(
    schema_id='hr-screening-compliance',
    verify_on=['run.completed']
)

thread = client.beta.threads.create()
run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id=assistant.id,
    extra_headers={'X-Vex-Webhook': webhook_url}
)

implementation.description: Define Vex schema blocking protected characteristics in screening decisions and requiring bias audit trails.

implementation.title: TypeScript Webhook Handler
implementation.description: Handle OpenAI run completion webhook, verify against Vex, and block non-compliant screening results.

caseStudyScenario.heading: OpenAI Assistants API in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
TechCo deployed an OpenAI Assistant for resume screening, processing 10,000 applications monthly. The Assistant used code interpreter to score candidates on 'leadership potential' and file search to reference internal hiring benchmarks. Without compliance checks, it began favoring candidates with profiles matching 'male software engineer' stereotypes—higher scores for GitHub activity patterns common among men and lower scores for resumes mentioning parental leave or community college attendance.

After three months, EEOC complaints emerged: female candidates and older applicants systematically under-ranked. NYC Local Law 144 violations surfaced when candidates weren't notified of AI usage. The company faced a class-action lawsuit demanding $4.5M in damages for 3,000 affected applicants.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex guardrails transformed compliance. Webhooks on run completion verified every screening batch against EEOC schemas, blocking 12% of outputs containing protected class proxies. Bias audit trails proved 98% compliance rate, satisfying annual reporting. Disclosure language was auto-enforced, eliminating notification violations. TechCo avoided litigation, reduced screening time by 40% with verified automation, and improved diverse hire rates by 25%.
